title: identity — retention windows
description: Identity retention is compliance-driven, not product-driven. Soft-deleted Identities carry a GDPR erasure hold-period (default 30 days) before hard delete. Email-verification tokens live 24 hours; password-reset tokens live 15 minutes; recovery codes never expire (regenerated on demand).
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 5(1)(e) (storage limitation) + Art. 17 (right to erasure) — identities purged past the hold-period
coppa 16 CFR § 312.10 (retention limitation) — identities resolved as minors receive stricter retention windows
ccpa § 1798.105 (right to delete) — 45-day statutory response window bounded by our 30-day hold + timely purge cycle
nist 800-63B § 4.2 (memorized secret lifecycle) — password_history retained ONLY as hashes, not plaintext

hot.days: while any inbound User / PlatformUser row references it

purge.trigger: manual soft-delete via `identity:erase` command OR compliance-officer HTTP endpoint
purge.action: sets deleted_at, keeps hashed credentials + PII for the hold-period
purge.notes: Row is NEVER hard-deleted while an inbound row exists. Users are cascade-erased BEFORE the Identity itself (design pattern: cascade downwards, purge upwards).

purge.trigger: deleted_at + hold_days < now() + no inbound rows remain
user regenerates OR disables MFA OR erasure hard-delete
purge.action: overwrite JSONB with new hashes OR set to NULL (MFA disabled)
purge.notes: Individual codes are single-use: once consumed, they're removed from the JSONB array in the same transaction as the login.

purge.trigger: new password change — evicts oldest entry when window is full
purge.action: JSONB array update inside the same transaction as the password change

purge.trigger: successful login OR AutoUnlockExpiredLockoutsJob after `identity.lockout.window_minutes` (default 15) of no activity

hold_period_extensions.description: The hold_days config can be extended (never shortened below the DEFAULT floor) by compliance officers when a request is under legal dispute. Extension is stored as a per-identity metadata flag (`retention_hold_extended_until`) that overrides the config default. PurgeSoftDeletedIdentitiesJob honours the per-identity override.
hold_period_extensions.how_to_extend: Via `identity:describe {id}` → `--set-hold-until=<date>` (proposed CLI extension — not shipped in Day 1 blueprint)
hold_period_extensions.audit_trail: Every extension is written to the compliance-audit log with the compliance officer as actor + free-form justification.
